name: Linda's Giant Cinnamon Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in the bread machine pan in the order given.
2. Set the crust control to light and start the regular cycle.
3. Keep an eye on the dough ball and ensure it is slightly stickier than usual but not so wet that it doesn't hold its shape.
4. Prepare a bread board by sprinkling it with a mixture of cinnamon and sugar (I used cinnamon maple sprinkle available from Sam's Club) using about 1/4 cup in all of the cinnamon mixture.
5. Have on hand 1/4 cup of raisins and (if desired) 1/4 cup pecans.
6. When the machine reaches the end of the first rise, remove the bread pan from the machine and close the lid.
7. Remove the dough from the pan and discard the paddle.
8. Roll the dough in the cinnamon mixture, stretching and elongating it until it is about 1 1/2 inches thick and quite long.
9. Work the cinnamon mixture into all of your bread "snake".
10. Spiral the "snake" back into the bread pan (if you don't have a spare pan or Finnish pan, wash your regular pan to remove any dough bits that may have clung to the sides) and sprinkle the nuts & raisins as you create a spiral loaf of bread.
11. Tuck the tail end back into the bread at the top so it doesn't come springing out when baking.
12. The bread is ready for the bake cycle.
The result is a lovely light cinnamon loaf that's great either sliced or pulled apart to eat. You could add a sugar glaze for a gift loaf, but we find it delicious without a glaze or even butter. Enjoy!
